AGR-001Grains & Agricultural

SRW Wheat

USDA #2 / 60 lbs/bu TW

USDA No. 2 Soft Red Winter (SRW) Wheat, 60 lbs/bu test weight, from US Gulf and Pacific Northwest terminals. CBOT Wheat benchmark at +$5.00/MT. Minimum 25,000 MT, FOB/CIF. SGS and USDA FGIS inspection. GAFTA terms.

Min. Volume
25,000 MT
Benchmark
CBOT Wheat

USDA #2 / 60 lbs/bu TW · Min 25,000 MT

Technical Specification

GradeUSDA No. 2 SRW
Test Weight60 lbs/bu min
Moisture13.5% max
OriginUS Gulf / Pacific Northwest
BenchmarkCBOT Wheat
Min. Volume25,000 MT
INCOTERMSFOB / CIF

Certifications & Compliance

USDA FGISGAFTA MemberFATF AMLPhytosanitary Certified
Responsible Desk
HOU / SGP Hub
agro@xrtgroup.com
Response SLA: <4 Business Hours

Soft Red Winter (SRW) Wheat is a versatile milling wheat used in cookies, crackers, cakes, and Asian noodle production. XRT Group sources USDA Grade No. 2 SRW directly from US Gulf of Mexico and Pacific Northwest export terminals, providing reliable supply to milling and food-manufacturing buyers worldwide.

Quality Specification

USDA Grade No. 2: minimum 60 lbs/bushel test weight, maximum 13.5% moisture, protein typically 9–11%. Full USDA FGIS phytosanitary and grade certificate issued with each cargo.

Logistics

Panamax and Handymax bulk loading at USGC (New Orleans, Mobile) and PNW (Portland, Kalama). Typical transit: 18–22 days USGC to Mediterranean, 25–30 days PNW to Southeast Asia.

Pricing

CBOT wheat futures-basis pricing. XRT's current indicative FOB premium: +$5.00/MT. CFR/CIF destinations available with transparent freight quotes.

Contract Terms

GAFTA 64 (FOB) / GAFTA 49 standard. LC issuance per UCP 600. SGS quantity and quality inspection at load port.

Typical Buyers

Flour mills, biscuit and cracker manufacturers, noodle producers, government strategic reserves.
